response.justification: The candidate is an experienced, versatile engineer with genuine language-tooling interest (test runners, assertion libraries, a C IDE at National Instruments) and long tenure across web, backend, and systems work. However, the role's core requirements — deep Java and Kotlin expertise, strong JVM internals knowledge, and solid Spring ecosystem experience — are largely absent. Java appears only peripherally in his history, Kotlin and Spring not at all, and his recent work centers on TypeScript/Node.js and DLang. The IDE internals passion is real but the required tech stack mismatch is too large for a mid-senior JVM tooling role.
strengths: Direct IDE development experience (NI LabWindows/CVI) plus self-built developer tooling (Trial test runner, Fluent-Asserts)
Proven low-latency, high-scale systems work handling millions of requests per second at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic
Strong craftsmanship culture: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, workshops and community teaching
concerns: No demonstrated Kotlin experience and only minor, dated Java exposure — the role's primary languages
No Spring, Quarkus, Hibernate, or enterprise JVM framework background
Recent focus is TypeScript/Node full-stack product work, distant from JVM internals and enterprise build/app-server tooling

key_factors.factor: IDE internals and dev-tooling track record
